title: API input validation accepts malformed payload
bug_description: A create endpoint accepts a malformed payload and returns success instead of rejecting the request with a validation error.
expected_behavior: Malformed input should return a 4xx validation error and should not create or mutate any record.
actual_behavior: The request returns success and downstream logic runs with partially invalid data.

preconditions: Local API server is running with test data loaded
A test user or auth fixture is available
steps: Send a request to the create endpoint with one required field missing and one field in the wrong type
Observe the response status and any downstream record creation side effects
reproduction.expected_behavior: The request should fail fast with a validation error and no side effects.
reproduction.actual_behavior: The request succeeds or fails too late after partial processing.

logs: API log shows request accepted and handler completed without validation rejection

manual_checks: Re-send the malformed request and confirm the endpoint now returns the expected validation error
Confirm no downstream record was created

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback on any hard-check failure or if valid requests start failing after the change
rollback.notes: Keep the last known passing validation branch or patch set ready for restore.

scope_notes: The fix should stay within request validation and the directly affected route. Do not refactor shared auth, persistence, or unrelated response formatting.

notes.root_cause_hint: Likely a missing schema check or a route path that bypasses the normal validator
notes.risk_hint: A broad fix could accidentally reject valid payload variants already used by clients
similar_bugs: missing required field not rejected in bulk-create mode
